 · 1991 · 1992 · 1993 · 1994 · 1995 · 1996 · 1997 · 1998 · 1999 · 2000 · 2001 · 2002 · 2003 · 2004 · 2005 · 2006 · 2007 · 2008 · 2009 · 2010 · 
 · 2011 · 2012 · 2013 · 2014 · 2015 · 2016 · 2017 · 2018 · 2019 · 2020 · 2021 · 2022 · 2023 · 2024 · 2025 · 2026 ·  		Live
Setlist
41 Live Performances of #41
#41 debuted in April 1995 as a Police-inspired jam with improvised lyrics and developed throughout 1995 until it was formalized on Crash twelve months later. It then grew from "album cut" (still with enough clout to surprise everyone on The Late Show With David Letterman) to the song that welcomed everyone back from COVID. It's a primary jam vehicle and enabled some of the most memorable guest spots in the band's history.

This timeline aims to tell the story of #41 in 41 live performances from the debut to the present (as of August 2023). The selections below are grouped into categories, and many versions fit into several categories. You will see these categories repeat in future timelines and some will be more specific to each table.

In the future, when we get to experience more performances worthy of the timeline, we'll add them below with a note. A complete timeline is more important than the final number listed.
# Category Length Show Date Venue Name and Location Personnel Jam Structure Release Notes 
First8:5504.07.95Cameron Indoor Stadium, Durham, NCBoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, Stefanfull bandfirst time played (and first encore)
Structure Change6:3004.22.95InCahoots, Oklahoma City, OKBoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, Stefanfull band > Roi (sax) > Dave (vocals) > vampfirst Roi sax solo, but not standard yet
Structure Change6:1008.11.95William Randolph Hearst Greek Theatre, Berkeley, CABoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, StefanBoyd > Dave (vocals) > vampfirst Boyd solo after the final chorus
Structure Change7:4912.29.95Patriot Center, Fairfax, VABoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, StefanBoyd & Roi (flute) > Roi (sax) > vampfirst performance to contain Roi's flute
First5:1302.01.96Indiana University Auditorium, Bloomington, INDave and TimTim > Dave (vocals) > vampfirst Dave & Tim, first time played as an opener, first full Crash lyrics (some in the late '95s)
Featured Release5:2102.06.96Center for Faith & Life, Decorah, IADave and TimTim > vamp
First4:1204.14.96Sweet Briar College Dell, Sweet Briar, VADave soloDave (vocals)first Dave solo
First05.23.96Große Freiheit 36, Hamburg, DEUBoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, Stefan(no recording)first full band opener - no extant recording
First05.29.96Barrumba, Turin, TO, ITABoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, Stefan(no recording)first #41 > Say Goodbye - no extant recording
Unique Performance3:5606.03.96The Jewish Mother, Virginia Beach, VADave soloDave (vocals)Dave played Andrew Winn's electric guitar during set break of an Agents of Good Roots show
Famous Guest Spots15:2612.31.96Hampton Coliseum, Hampton, VABoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, Stefan, Béla Fleck, Futureman, Paul McCandless, Victor WootenBoyd > Vic > "Sojourn of Arjuna" > Béla > Paul (piccolo) >
vamp > Dave (guitar) > vamp		first with all of the Flecktones; first Sojourn of Arjuna
First14:0806.08.97The Meadows Music Theatre, Hartford, CTBoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, Stefan, Béla Fleck, JeffBoyd > Roi (flute) > Roi (sax) > Béla "Sojourn of Arjuna" >
Jeff & Béla > Jeff > "Sojourn of Arjuna" > vamp		first guest appearance by Jeff Coffin
Unique Performance3:5606.09.97Ed Sullivan Theatre, New York, NYBoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, StefanBoyd > Roi (sax) > vampThe Late Show With David Letterman
Famous Guest Spots13:1108.26.98Coral Sky Amphitheatre, West Palm Beach, FLBoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, Stefan, Herbie Hancock, Paul JacksonBoyd > Roi (flute) > Herbie > full band > vamp
First27:2811.21.98The Crown, Cincinnati, OHBoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, Stefan, Béla Fleck, Futureman, Jeff, Tim, Victor WootenBoyd > Vic > Roi (sax) > Jeff > Béla & Vic >
"Sojourn of Arjuna" > vamp > Tim > Carter & Futureman		first time played as an encore closer; at the time was the longest documented song in DMB history
Featured Release20:5112.08.98Worcester's Centrum Centre, Worcester, MABoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, Stefan, Béla Fleck, Butch, Jeff, TimBoyd > Roi (sax) > Béla > Jeff > "Sojourn of Arjuna" >
vamp w/ Dave (vocals)		released on original The Warehouse 5 and Live Trax Vol. 1
Featured Release9:4209.11.99Continental Airlines Arena, East Rutherford, NJBoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, Stefan, ButchBoyd > Roi (flute) > Roi (sax) > vamp
First13:3312.03.00Target Center, Minneapolis, MNBoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, StefanBoyd > Roi (flute) > Roi (sax) > Dave (vocals) (w/ "Everyday") > vampfirst "Everyday" outro (after improv lyrics - no pauses yet)
Famous Guest Spots21:0405.15.01Blockbuster Desert Sky Pavilion, Phoenix, AZBoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, Stefan, Eric Krasno, Neal Evans, Sam KiningerBoyd > Sam, Kraz & Neal foundation > Sam > Kraz >
Neal > Roi (sax) > "Everyday" > intros > vamp
Famous Guest Spots29:0307.25.01AmSouth Amphitheatre, Antioch, TNBoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, Stefan, Béla Fleck, Butch, Futureman, JeffBoyd > Roi (sax) > Bela > Jeff with "Sojourn of Arjuna" >
Carter & Futureman > Dave improv > "Everyday" > vamp		at the time was the longest documented song in DMB history
First32:0304.20.02Corel Centre, Ottawa, ON, CANBoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, Stefan, Béla Fleck, Butch, Futureman, Jeff, Victor WootenBoyd > Roi > Béla & Vic > "Sojourn of Arjuna" > Jeff >
Carter & Futureman w/ "Sojourn of Arjuna" vamp >
Dave (spoken word) > full band w/ Dave (vocals) > vamp		first time played as a closer; currently the longest documented song in DMB history
Structure Change10:0604.27.02Allstate Arena, Rosemont, ILBoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, Stefan, ButchBoyd > Roi (flute) > Roi (sax) > vampfinal performance to contain Roi's flute solo
Unique Performance5:0110.24.02Benaroya Hall, Seattle, WADave soloDave improv lyricsDave improvs a set of lyrics after repeating the first chorus
First15:0309.11.05Red Rocks Amphitheatre, Morrison, COBoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, Stefan, Butch, RashawnBoyd > Roi (sax) > Rashawn > "Everyday" > vampfirst time played with Rashawn

Famous Guest Spots13:4410.02.07Hollywood Bowl, Hollywood, CABoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, Stefan, Butch, John Mayer, RashawnBoyd > John > "Everyday" > vamp
Structure Change11:5305.31.08Post-Gazette Pavilion, Burgettstown, PABoyd, Carter, Dave, Roi, Stefan, Rashawn, TimBoyd > Tim > "Everyday" > vampTim takes over for Roi
Structure Change14:1507.02.08Walnut Creek Amphitheatre, Raleigh, NCBoyd, Carter, Dave, Stefan, Jeff, Rashawn, TimBoyd > Tim > "Sojourn of Arjuna" > Jeff > crowd "Everyday" > vampsax solo back when Jeff fills in for Roi
Featured Release20:5806.26.09O2 Academy, Brixton, London, ENGBoyd, Carter, Dave, Stefan, Jeff, Rashawn, TimBoyd > "Sojourn of Arjuna" > Jeff > Tim (w/ "I'll Be

The Six Million Dollar Man
Structure Change15:5909.18.10Wrigley Field, Chicago, ILBoyd, Carter, Dave, Stefan, Jeff, Rashawn, TimBoyd > Jeff > Tim > vampfinal version with standard sax solo

First12:0908.31.12The Gorge Amphitheatre, George, WABoyd, Carter, Dave, Stefan, Jeff, Rashawn, TimBoyd > Tim > Boyd w/ "Sojourn of Arjuna" vampopened the 41st show at The Gorge (done at other venues since)
